title: Tangy Chewy Australian Cheese Stir-fry
description: A tangy and chewy one-pot dish featuring cheese in an easy-to-make, sugar-free-friendly meal.
instructions: Cut chicken into bite-sized pieces. Slice bell peppers and onion.
In a large skillet or frying pan, heat butter or olive oil over medium-high heat.
Add garlic cloves and cook for about 1 minute until fragrant.
Add sliced onions and stir-fry for another 2-3 minutes.
Add chicken pieces and stir fry for an additional 5 minutes, breaking them into smaller chunks as they start to brown.
Stir in bell peppers and continue cooking for a few more minutes until all vegetables are slightly tender but still have some crunch.
Pour tomato paste over the veggies and mix well. Reduce heat to medium-low.
Slowly add white wine vinegar (or lemon juice) and maple syrup, stirring continuously. The mixture will thicken.
Once the sauce has reduced to a rich consistency, stir in grated cheddar cheese until completely melted and smoothly incorporated.
Season with black pepper and salt to taste. Let simmer for an extra 2-3 minutes to let flavors meld together.
Garnish with fresh thyme leaves if desired. Serve hot as a side dish or main course over steamed rice.
